meanings.definition: Lied deliberately after making a legal or formal promise to tell the truth, especially in a court of law.
examples: The witness perjured himself during the trial.
She was accused of perjuring herself to protect her friend.
Anyone who knowingly perjures themselves in court can face punishment.
The lawyer proved that the official had perjured himself under oath.

meanings.definition: Having lied under oath or given false legal testimony.
examples: The judge rejected the perjured testimony.
He was exposed as a perjured witness.
The case collapsed because of perjured statements.
